questions.explanation: "Changes" is plural — "There have been significant changes."

questions.sentence: Which sentence is correct?
options: There is five members in the group.
There are five member in the group.
There are five members in the group.
questions.answer: There are five members in the group.
questions.explanation: "Five members" is plural present — "There are five members."

questions.sentence: Choose the correct sentence:
options: There is a lot of evidences.
There are a lot of evidence.
There is a lot of evidence.
There were a lot of evidences.
questions.answer: There is a lot of evidence.
questions.explanation: "Evidence" is uncountable — "There is a lot of evidence."

questions.sentence: Fill in: There ___ many visitors in the museum.
questions.explanation: "Visitors" is plural present — "There are many visitors."

questions.sentence: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: "Errors" is countable and plural — "There are many errors." ("Much" is for uncountable.)

questions.sentence: Choose the correct future negative:
options: There will not be any problems.
questions.answer: There will not be any problems.
questions.explanation: "There will not be any problems" is the correct future negative.

questions.sentence: Fill in: There ___ no clear answer to this question.
questions.explanation: "Answer" is singular — "There is no clear answer."

questions.sentence: Which sentence is correct?
options: There is many lessons to learn.
There are many lessons to learn.
There were a lot of lesson.
There is a lot of lessons.
questions.answer: There are many lessons to learn.
questions.explanation: "Lessons" is plural — "There are many lessons."

questions.sentence: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.explanation: "Accidents" is plural — "There have been many accidents."

questions.sentence: Fill in: There ___ a long discussion in the boardroom.
questions.explanation: "Discussion" is singular past — "There was a long discussion."

questions.sentence: Which sentence is correct?
options: There is a plenty of resources.
There were a plenty of resources.
questions.explanation: "Plenty of resources" — "There are plenty of resources."

questions.sentence: Choose the right form: There ___ been a lot of complaints.
questions.explanation: "Complaints" is plural — "There have been a lot of complaints."

questions.sentence: Fill in: There ___ no water available during the drought.
questions.explanation: "Water" is uncountable/singular past — "There was no water available."

questions.sentence: Which sentence is correct?
options: There will be a lot of traffics.
There will be a lot of traffic.
There will are a lot of traffic.
There will were a lot of traffics.
questions.answer: There will be a lot of traffic.
questions.explanation: "Traffic" is uncountable — "There will be a lot of traffic."

questions.sentence: Choose the correct sentence:
options: There is a lot of furnitures.
There are a lot of furnitures.
There is a lot of furniture.
There are a lot of furniture.
questions.answer: There is a lot of furniture.
questions.explanation: "Furniture" is uncountable — "There is a lot of furniture."

questions.sentence: Fill in: There ___ many flights cancelled due to the storm.
questions.explanation: "Flights" is plural past — "There were many flights cancelled."

questions.sentence: Which is the correct negative question?
questions.explanation: "Space" is uncountable — "Is there no more space?" is correct.

questions.sentence: Choose the correct sentence:
options: There is a many tasks today.
questions.explanation: "Tasks" is plural — "There are many tasks today."

questions.sentence: Fill in: There ___ been rapid growth in this sector.
questions.explanation: "Growth" is uncountable/singular — "There has been rapid growth."
